mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth is below 0.1pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series decouples, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and operational momentum, generating short-term positive market sentiment and upward price pressure over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge (Sharpe < 0.5) when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy and elevated inflation creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and raises discount rates, systematically producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate when both conditions are met, or the interaction effect vanishes when inflation falls below 2.5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings expansion, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of periods when GDPC1 growth is positive, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.15 in rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor market absorption and rising household income, fueling consumer expenditure and driving positive equity market performance over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 200k in a single month
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns when payrolls surge alongside rising unemployment duration, or predictive accuracy falls below 52% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial output expansion consistently outpaces retail sales, it signals inventory overhang and weakening demand absorption, leading to corporate margin compression and negative forward equity returns over the quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > RSAFS growth for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive despite the divergence, or the mechanism fails to predict downturns when inventory-to-sales ratios remain stable.
